title: Outeniqua Primary proud of top 20 achievers
bodyText:
SCHOOLS NEWS - Outeniqua Primary School celebrated its academic top 20 of the second term with pride and lauded each learner for their hard work and commitment.

See the following photographs below:

Grade Four. Back: Alex Nel, Lisa Stark, André Pieterse, Aninka Terblanche, Emma Saaiman, Rosco van der Merwe, Larissa Strydom, Kieran Opperman and Connor Simpson. Middle: Linnea Bond, Deborah Snyders, Julé Jansen van Vuuren, Amo O’Kennedy, Katherine Lamprecht, Bea van Rooyen and Carlisle Kaywitz. Front: Liam Swart, Philip du Plessis, Alex Botes and Ernan Human.

Grade Seven. Back: Kayla van Rooyen, Ané Marias, Gia Fourie, Joshua Soekoe, Ben Schneider, Hugo Stark, Luke Liebenberg, Ally van Rooyen and Mia Brandt. Middle: Lené Koen, Elné van der Merwe, Lara Rossouw, Lia Schmidt, Jani Gertenbach, Miahlize Kamener and Mieke Koen. Front: Zanelle Kruger, Brooklyn-Cay Leen and Zené Hugo. Absent: Isabelle Nagel.

Grade Six. Back: Christopher Chowels, Gracie Barkhuizen, Jané Vivier, Mia Kemp, Amelié Smith, Nhea du Toit, Lenka Badenhorst, Marloe Jansen, Joy Windvogel, Aimee-Lee Mentoor and Nikolas O’Kennedy. Front: Mika Lötze, Emma Kroon, Milé Kroon, Henno Claassen, Francois Bruwer, Christiaan Mundey, Ashleigh Kock, Rupert Human and Idolan de Klerk.

Grade Five. Back: Zoey Kotzé, Payton Morris, Han-Marie Koen, Danika Janse van Rensburg, Fanie Spies, Stella Paton, Lily Schutte, Ben Bond and Miane Cornelius. Front: Erich van Rensburg, Abigail van der Merwe, Milay Wilkens, Logan Swanepoel, Deon van der Linde, Ruben Crause, Connor Jacobs, Adriaan Coetzee, Ebeth Murray and Anja Pieterse. Absent: Niqueta Roux.

‘We bring you the latest Garden Route, Hessequa, Karoo news’
